task_completion.description: Calculate sleep efficiency for the tracking data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column or set of cells showing calculated sleep efficiency (Time Asleep divided by Time in Bed). The value for the first row should be approximately 0.9067 or 90.67.

task_completion.description: Format the sleep efficiency values as percentages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calculated sleep efficiency values are visually formatted with a '%' symbol (e.g., 90.67%).
A cell explicitly displaying the calculated average sleep duration, which should be 6.7 or 6.70.

task_completion.description: Count the number of insufficient sleep nights.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A cell explicitly displaying the count of insufficient sleep nights, which should be exactly 9.

task_completion.description: Apply conditional formatting to highlight poor sleep quality.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Specific cells containing poor sleep metrics (e.g., sleep duration < 7 hours, or low sleep efficiency) are highlighted with a distinct background color (such as red or yellow).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a spreadsheet graphical user interface, not a plain text editor or terminal output.

integrity.description: Verify the calculations are performed alongside the actual dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original sleep tracking data columns (Date, Bedtime, Wake Time, Time in Bed, Time Asleep, etc.) are visible in the spreadsheet alongside the new calculations.

integrity.description: Verify that highlighting is applied to the cells themselves.
integrity.visual_evidence: The background color of the data cells is changed to indicate poor sleep quality, rather than the agent just typing the word 'poor' next to the data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 14 nights of data. The correct average sleep duration is 6.70 hours. The correct count of insufficient sleep nights (<7 hours) is exactly 9 nights. The correct sleep efficiency for the first night is approximately 90.67%.
